commit.message:
fix(py): stream_iter() now propagates system_prompt and tools (#80)

stream_iter() was creating AgentStream with only temperature/max_tokens,
using the low-level stream_generate() instead of agent_stream(). This
caused system_prompt, tools, and all other agent options to be silently
dropped during streaming.

Changes:
- AgentStream.__init__: accepts agent_name and options dict
- AgentStream.__aiter__: uses agent_stream (with full options) when
  options are provided; falls back to stream_generate for legacy compat
- Agent.stream_iter: now passes _build_options() to AgentStream
- StreamEvent.from_json: also checks 'delta' field (not just 'data'),
  and captures 'text' from 'done' events

* feat: add fuzz testing for protocol crate decode paths

Four fuzz targets using cargo-fuzz (libfuzzer):
- fuzz_decode_frame: raw bytes -> decode_frame()
- fuzz_node_message: msg_type + CBOR -> NodeMessage::decode()
- fuzz_gateway_message: msg_type + CBOR -> GatewayMessage::decode()
- fuzz_program_image: CBOR -> ProgramImage::decode()

These test that the protocol crate never panics on arbitrary input,
which matters because the gateway processes frames from the untrusted
radio channel. HMAC verification catches most garbage, but the CBOR
decoder must also be robust against malformed payloads from a
compromised node.

CI: separate fuzz job using nightly toolchain, 30 seconds per target
(2 minutes total). Runs on every push to main and every PR.

fix: replace unsafe ExecException cast with type guard in execute()

The catch block was using `err as ExecException` — an unsafe cast that
bypasses TypeScript's unknown-type narrowing. Replace with a structural
type guard:

  const execErr =
    typeof err === 'object' && err !== null && 'code' in err
      ? (err as ExecException)
      : null;

Then access all ExecException fields (code, stdout, stderr, signal, killed)
via optional chaining with the same `typeof` guards that were already in
place, falling back to safe defaults when execErr is null.

In practice exec() always rejects with ExecException on command failure, but
the null branch makes the code safe for any unexpected error shape.

Fixes 🔴 Critical finding from TypeScript review (step_19 workflow log).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix scaled KGP image shifting during occlusion

Integer division truncation in clip coordinate mapping caused adjacent
fragments to not tile perfectly. E.g., 128px in 30 cells: a fragment
at offset 10 got clipW=floor(5*128/30)=21, but the next fragment at
offset 15 started at clipX=floor(15*128/30)=64, leaving a 1px gap

higher layer (proper slicing).

Fix: save registry.CurrentLayer right after PushKgpLayer for the
deferred occluder registration. This ensures the occluder layer
accurately reflects the global ordering.

Updated test to verify the image is actually sliced (more placements
after menu open than before).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix KGP window disappearing after drag + terminal resize

On resize, \x1b[2J clears text cells but KGP placements are terminal-level
state that persists until explicitly deleted. After dragging a KGP-containing
window, old placements at stale positions remained visible and interfered
with the re-rendered layout.

Fix: send KGP delete-all (\x1b_Ga=d,d=a,q=2\x1b\) before screen clear on
resize. Added HasEverTransmitted flag to KgpPlacementTracker that survives
Reset() to correctly detect when cleanup is needed.

Added two new integration tests:
- KGP window drag+resize survival (with KGP image)
- Non-KGP window drag+resize control test

docs: update ADR-001 with Foundry agent client-side tools findings (#6)

Document findings from testing a Foundry-hosted agent with client-side
function tools (foundry_agent.py):

- Client-side function tools on Foundry agents work — the Agent Framework
  registers definitions, intercepts calls locally, and submits results
- Server-side MCP tool events (e.g. Foundry IQ) leak through the AG-UI
  stream, causing phantom tool_call_end errors that could not be resolved
- This is the primary reason the project uses Option 3 (Foundry agent as
  tool) rather than a Foundry-hosted orchestrator
- Added 'what would unblock' criteria for future re-evaluation
- Updated comparison matrix with new rows for these findings

Fix code quality issues and improve test reliability (#17)

* Fix code quality issues and improve test reliability

- Add missing dependencies to root package.json (@opencode-ai/plugin,
  @opencode-ai/sdk, @ai-eng-system/core)
- Fix build.ts to properly handle TEST_ROOT environment variable,
  skipping sync operations that access paths outside the test sandbox
- Fix loadConfig.ts to read TEST_ROOT at call time via getRoot() function
  instead of module load time (both src/ and packages/cli/src/)
- Fix task executor timeout using process groups (detached: true +
  process.kill(-pid)) for reliable child process termination
- Fix integration tests hardcoded path (/home/vitruvius/...) to use
  ORIGINAL_ROOT from process.cwd()
- Remove process.chdir() calls from integration and installation tests
  that caused test pollution during concurrent execution
- Fix build test error pattern assertions to match actual error messages
- Add TEST_ROOT isolation to config loading and CLI tests
- Increase task executor test timeouts for CI stability
- Clean up stale files: build.ts.tmp, .bak backup files
- Update .gitignore: add .ai-eng/runs/, .opencode/opencode.db*, *.bak

Test results improved from 769 pass / 84 fail to 794 pass / 59 fail
(25 fewer failures, ~30% reduction). Remaining failures are caused by
concurrent test pollution (shared process.env.HOME) and pass individually.

commit.message:
feat(m2): thin end-to-end slice — engine, headless mode, game loop

* feat(m2): thin end-to-end slice — engine, headless mode, game loop

Implements M2 (cryptd-7uw): the minimal slice that proves the full
L1 architecture is wired up and runnable end-to-end without LLM.

New packages:
- internal/engine: deterministic rules engine (NewGame, Move, Look)
  with typed errors NoExitError and LockedError
- internal/interpreter: RulesInterpreter maps text commands to
  EngineActions (go <dir>, look, quit and aliases)
- internal/narrator: TemplateNarrator produces fixed-template
  narrations for all event types
- internal/renderer: CLIRenderer reads stdin / writes stdout
- internal/game: Loop wires engine+interpreter+narrator+renderer;
  drives full pipeline until quit or context cancel

CLI:
- crypt headless --scenario <id>: runs headless mode; respects
  CRYPT_SCENARIO_DIR env var for scenario lookup path

Tests:
- Unit tests in all packages (no build tag)
- Integration tests in internal/game with adversarial fakes covering
  ctx cancel, channel close, quit event, narrate/render error paths
- E2E test (build tag e2e) compiles binary and runs scripted session
- Coverage ≥ 90% on all M2 packages (engine 93.8%, game 92.1%,
  narrator/renderer 100%, interpreter 95%)

Fixtures:
- testdata/scripts/minimal-run.yaml: M2 acceptance script

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(m2): address review — state corruption, quit narration, context, types

Addresses all issues surfaced by Cursor Bugbot review:

- engine: validate destination room before mutating GameState so a
  failed Move never leaves state corrupted (state-before-error-check)
- engine: hidden connections now return NoExitError (undiscoverable
  until revealed) instead of LockedError
- engine/doc.go: remove premature 'combat, inventory' claims
- game/loop: route renderer-emitted quit events through dispatch so
  quit narration ('Farewell, adventurer.') is rendered before exit
- game/loop: render quit narration after dispatch returns quit event
- game/loop: map LockedError -> 'locked_door' event, NoExitError ->
  'no_exit' event so narrators can give specific feedback
- narrator: add 'locked_door' and 'no_exit' template narrations
- renderer/cli: move stdin scan into goroutine (startOnce) so
  Render never blocks on input and ctx cancellation is honoured
- cmd/crypt: use filepath.Join for cross-platform path assembly
- cmd/crypt: rename usage strings from 'crypt' to 'cryptd'
- e2e: build binary as 'cryptd' matching documented binary name
- testdata/scripts: fix header comment (no --script flag exists)
- tests: remove unused errRenderer; add locked_door/no_exit/unknown
  dispatch coverage; use select+timeout in renderer tests

Coverage: engine 91.2%, game 97.6%, narrator/renderer 100% (total 91.6%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(engine): hidden connections not leaked in exitList

exitList filtered over all room.Connections without checking type,
causing hidden connections to appear in LookResult.Exits and
MoveResult.Exits — contradicting the engine's own policy that hidden
connections are undiscoverable until revealed.

Now exitList skips entries where conn.Type == "hidden", consistent
with Move which already returns NoExitError for hidden directions.

Added tests:
- TestLook_HiddenConnectionsNotInExits: verifies north (hidden) absent
- TestMove_HiddenIsNoExit: verifies hidden direction returns NoExitError
Updated minimal.yaml: added secret_room with hidden north exit from entrance.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(game): propagate internal engine errors from dispatch instead of swallowing them as no_exit

NoExitError and LockedError are expected game conditions mapped to distinct
events. Any other error from Engine.Move indicates state/scenario corruption
and must propagate so callers can surface a diagnostic.

Added TestLoop_MoveInternalErrorPropagates to cover the new branch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Clarify skillpm’s position relative to GitHub CLI/Claude plugin specs (#57)

Users asked what emerging GitHub CLI and Claude plugin specs imply for
skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- **Documentation: plugin-spec positioning**
  - Added a new section to `README.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs (GitHub CLI / Claude Code)`
  - Added a matching section to `docs/index.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs`
  - Both sections explicitly state:
    - plugin specs and Agent Skills are complementary, not replacements
- skillpm’s model is unchanged (`npm` for deps + `skills` for wiring +
`add-mcp` for MCP config)

- **Docs consistency**
- Kept language aligned across repository README and docs homepage so
users get the same answer regardless of entry point.

Example excerpt added:

```md
- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.
```

fix: exclude logs/summaries from workflow doc scan; sync version

- Add structure.exclude_paths to .workflow-config.yaml:
    - .ai_workflow/logs      (prompt logs must not be analyzed)
    - .ai_workflow/summaries (workflow summaries are not project docs)
  Prevents log-file markdown from appearing in the files-to-analyze list
  and stops the broken-link scanner from crawling AI-generated log content,
  which produced false-positive broken-ref findings (e.g. docs/errors.md
  incorrectly reported as missing; ./api/README.md links inside log text
  misidentified as broken project references).
